generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance StructuralHealthSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor capturing structural integrity metrics (e.g., vibration, strain) for real-time monitoring of building components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT sensor capturing environmental parameters (e.g., temperature, humidity, air quality) for building performance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart meter measuring real-time energy consumption across building systems (HVAC, lighting, equipment)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motion/presence sensor tracking occupancy patterns and space utilization metrics"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACSystemTelemetry : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System (BCS) telemetry providing operational status of HVAC components (e.g., chiller plants, AHUs)"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACTelemetryDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance HVACTelemetryDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACSystemTelemetry
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance EnrichedBimModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Foundational static model integrating geometric, spatial, and functional data from BIM tools (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) with semantic consistency across lifecycle phases"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDtTransformationEnabler, DittoDeviceAbstractionEnabler
]

instance StructuralAnalysisModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ANSYS-based finite element model for structural behavior simulation and validation of DT predictions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance EnergySimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "EnergyPlus-based model for HVAC system performance simulation under varying environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated live data from IoT sensors and BCS telemetry synchronized between physical and digital systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalConditionDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, HVACTelemetryDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoDeviceAbstractionEnabler, TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler, GraphDatabaseEnabler
]

instance CustomServiceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Domain-specific data integrated through modular extension points for analytics and visualization services"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationPlatformEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BimToDtTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) framework automating extraction of structured DT artifacts from enriched BIM models"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance DittoDeviceAbstractionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto framework providing device and asset abstraction with dynamic twin state management and event-driven updates"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, OptimizationService
]

instance EclipseHonoMessagingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT-based messaging broker (Eclipse Hono) for secure, scalable IoT data ingestion with high-throughput sensor streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Domain-specific solvers (ANSYS, EnergyPlus) for physical behavior modeling and DT prediction validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService, OptimizationService
]

instance VisualizationPlatformEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine platform for real-time 3D rendering with VR/AR interactions and immersive analytics overlay"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationAndSimulationService, CollaborativeWorkflowService
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "InfluxDB-based time-series database for storing and querying structured operational data with low-latency access"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance GraphDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Neo4j-based graph database for managing relationships between heterogeneous data sources in AECO ecosystems"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, CollaborativeWorkflowService
]

instance InteroperabilityProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized protocols (OPC UA, REST APIs, RDF/OWL) enabling data exchange between legacy systems, IoT devices, and DT components"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, CustomServiceIntegrationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous tracking of building performance metrics (energy, structural integrity, occupancy) with data-driven insights"
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, AnomalyDetectionAction
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Proactive maintenance and failure prediction through runtime data integration and simulation-based forecasting"
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationInsight, FailurePredictionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operational efficiency improvements through simulation-based decision support for energy use and resource allocation"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationInsight, CostReductionInsight
]

instance VisualizationAndSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization of building performance under varying conditions with VR/AR interactions"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalImpactInsight, DesignValidationInsight
]

instance CollaborativeWorkflowService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-disciplinary stakeholder engagement platform supporting lifecycle collaboration across AECO domains"
    DTDFVocab:provides StakeholderCommunicationInsight, WorkflowAutomationAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Modular extension framework enabling integration of domain-specific plugins and analytics services"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight, PluginExtensionAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard showing KPIs for energy consumption, structural health, and occupancy patterns with historical trends"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert generation for detected deviations from normal operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance schedule with component-specific recommendations based on degradation analysis"
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Probabilistic failure forecasts for critical building systems with confidence intervals"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized HVAC control strategies reducing energy consumption by 15-25% through simulation-based validation"
]

instance CostReductionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Lifecycle cost analysis comparing current operations against optimized scenarios with ROI projections"
]

instance EnvironmentalImpactInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visualized carbon footprint analysis under different occupancy and environmental scenarios"
]

instance DesignValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-based validation of design modifications before physical implementation"
]

instance StakeholderCommunicationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shared digital workspace with role-based access for architects, facility managers, and operators"
]

instance WorkflowAutomationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated task assignment and approval routing for maintenance and operational workflows"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Domain-specific analytics results from integrated plugins (e.g., acoustic modeling, indoor air quality)"
]

instance PluginExtensionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual deployment of new service plugins through standardized extension points"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **bidirectional control flows** from the digital twin to physical infrastructure, primarily through the interaction of **Facility Managers** who utilize the deployed DT for **real-time monitoring and control**. Control commands are executed via the DT Framework, which acts as a central mediator for **device-level adjustments** in the physical building. While specific command types (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting controls) are not enumerated, the system supports **dynamic operational adjustments** without requiring full redeployment, as enabled by **runtime configuration management**. Validation and authentication mechanisms for control messages are not explicitly described, though the framework’s **modular architecture** implies support for **standardized security protocols** to ensure authorized access. Feedback systems are inferred to exist for **closed-loop control**, though specifics (e.g., confirmation messages, error handling) are absent. Conflict resolution strategies are not detailed, but the system’s emphasis on **interoperability** suggests mechanisms to reconcile discrepancies between digital and physical states."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling dynamic operational monitoring and control. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, the framework prioritizes **low-latency bidirectional data exchange** to maintain temporal alignment between the physical and digital domains. The system supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments to operational parameters without full redeployment, which implies adaptability to varying time scales for different services (e.g., sensor data aggregation, predictive analytics, or control commands). The temporal granularity is implicitly tied to the **operational needs of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain**, where real-time and near-real-time interactions are critical for facility management and building control engineers. The framework bridges static BIM representations with dynamic operational requirements, suggesting a hybrid time-scale approach where **high-frequency synchronization** is required for critical systems (e.g., HVAC, security) while lower-frequency updates may suffice for non-critical analytics or reporting services."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **coordinated, role-based orchestration pattern** centered around a **centralized DT Framework** that acts as a unifying hub for bidirectional data exchange and life cycle management. The architecture is **component-based**, with clear delineation of roles: **Building Control Engineers** (responsible for device modeling and deployment), **Facility Managers** (interacting with operational twins), and **DT Engineers** (extending capabilities via service integration). The framework’s **modular architecture** supports **standardized extension points**, enabling plug-and-play integration of custom services and analytics while maintaining system-wide coherence. **Resource management** is implicit through the **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments (e.g., reallocating computational resources for high-priority services) without full redeployment. The system-wide coordination is achieved via **enriched BIM-to-operational DT workflows**, ensuring seamless transitions from static modeling to dynamic operational twins, and addressing challenges in interoperability, scalability, and maintainability across the AECO domain."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers within the BIM environment, creating a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components, ensuring compatibility with downstream DT artifacts.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract structured representations from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This phase bridges the static BIM model and the dynamic DT by structuring data for real-time synchronization.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling **real-time data exchange** between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase ensures continuous synchronization of system states, supporting operational monitoring and control.
  4. **Runtime Operation and Extension**: The DT operates with **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control, while its **modular architecture** (with standardized extension points) allows Facility Managers and DT Engineers to integrate **custom services and analytics**. This phase supports evolutionary DT functionality, adapting to changing operational requirements.
  **Transition Management**: The framework ensures seamless transitions between phases via automated model transformations and standardized interfaces, reducing manual effort. **Lifecycle Governance** is implicit through role-based responsibilities (e.g., Building Control Engineers for modeling, Facility Managers for monitoring, DT Engineers for extensions), ensuring accountability and alignment with operational needs."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework enforces **fidelity requirements** by ensuring **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital twins via **real-time data exchange capabilities**. Validation and verification are implicitly supported through the **model-driven transformation pipeline**, which extracts structured representations from BIM-enriched models and generates deployable DT artifacts. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated by the **semantic foundation** (e.g., explicit modeling of BCS components) and **automated artifact generation**, reducing manual errors. **Error detection** is facilitated by the **modular architecture**, allowing custom services to integrate validation logic (e.g., anomaly detection in sensor data). **Quality assurance** is embedded in the life-cycle process, spanning from **device modeling** (by Building Control Engineers) to **runtime operation** (monitored by Facility Managers). The framework’s reliance on **standardized extension points** ensures extensibility for domain-specific validation methods (e.g., physics-based simulations or statistical validation)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **centralized network architecture**, with the DT Framework serving as the primary hub for **bidirectional data and control communication**. The framework supports **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, leveraging technologies such as **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT data ingestion) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for digital twin state management) to ensure seamless integration. While specific communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, CoAP) are not explicitly named, the system is designed to accommodate **low-latency, high-reliability data exchange** to meet real-time operational demands. Network topology is implied to be **scalable and modular**, aligning with the framework’s extension points for custom services. Redundancy mechanisms are not detailed, but the requirement for **scalability** suggests fault-tolerant designs. Security protocols are not specified beyond the general need for **accessibility and maintainability** across varying technical expertise levels."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed using a **modular architecture** that supports **runtime configuration management**, enabling adjustments without full redeployment. The framework’s design emphasizes **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, though specific hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise) are not explicitly mentioned. Computational and storage requirements are implied to be **flexible and adaptable**, aligning with the system’s dynamic operational needs. Deployment models are not detailed beyond the **centralized DT Framework** acting as a hub for BIM-enriched models and real-time data. Availability and reliability assurances are addressed through **low-overhead updates** and **maintainability** features, though specific mechanisms (e.g., fault tolerance, load balancing) are not provided. Visualization tools like **Unreal Engine** are referenced for immersive interfaces but do not specify hosting infrastructure constraints."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework ensures **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through:
  - **Standardized extension points** for integrating custom services, analytics, and third-party tools.
  - **Bidirectional data exchange** with external systems, leveraging **real-time protocols** (e.g., Eclipse Hono for IoT data ingestion, Eclipse Ditto for digital twin state management).
  - **BIM tool integration** (e.g., for device modeling and initial deployment) to bridge static representations with dynamic operational needs.
  The system does not specify **external dependencies** (e.g., Autodesk Revit, ArchiCAD) or **protocols** (e.g., OData, OPC UA) beyond the mentioned technologies. Interoperability is framed as a **non-functional requirement (NF_X)** addressing scalability, maintainability, and accessibility across diverse technical expertise levels, with a focus on **enterprise system integration** via the central DT Framework."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **AECO-specific systems and standards**, primarily leveraging **BIM (Building Information Modeling)** as the foundational representation for DT deployment. **Interoperability** is ensured through **model-driven transformations** that extract structured data from BIM, enabling seamless integration with downstream DT components. While the text does not explicitly mention **IFC**, **OPC UA**, **MQTT**, or **ISO 19650**, the reliance on BIM implies compliance with **industry-standard BIM data exchange formats** and **semantic consistency** requirements. The modular architecture enforces **standardized extension points** for custom services, promoting **interoperability** with third-party tools. However, no specific **certification requirements** or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., ISO/IEC 19000-series) are referenced in the provided documents."
]
